title: Predictive and diagnostic methods for prostate cancer
abstract: The present invention generally relates to a method of diagnosing, prognosing and treating prostate cancer patients. Particularly, the present invention relates to a method of selecting patients with castration resistant prostate cancer (CrPC) for combination therapy comprising a selective dipeptidyl peptidase inhibitor and a PD-1 axis antagonist. The present invention provides a computational approach to identifying potential patients for CrPC. The present invention also relates to a method of treating castration resistant prostate cancer (CrPC) patients with said combination therapy.
claims:
Claims Claims: 1. A computer implemented method for identifying a cancer patient segment in a dataset comprising: (a) receiving a dataset of a biological sample from the cancer patient; (b) determining/analyzing the amplification and/or expression level of the genes including selective dipeptidyl peptidases consisting of DPP-8, DPP-9 or FAP and programmed death ligand-1 (PDL-1) or CD274 in the dataset of the biological sample received from the cancer patient; and, (c) identifying the cancer patient segment in the dataset, wherein the amplification and/or expression level of selective dipeptidyl peptidases consisting of DPP-8, DPP-9 or FAP and programmed death ligand-1 (PDL-1) or CD274 exceeds a predetermined threshold level. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the type of cancer is castration resistant prostate cancer (CrPC). 3. The method of claim 2, wherein the castration resistant prostate cancer (CrPC) comprises small cell cancer, large cell cancer, neuroendocrine prostate cancer (NEPC), castration resistant prostate cancer neuroendocrine (CrPC-NE) and castration resistant prostate cancer (CrPC) pre- treated with Abiraterone and/or Enzalutamide. 4. The method of claim 1, wherein the determining the amplification and/or expression level of the genes comprises an amplification assay, a hybridization assay, an immunoassay, immunohistochemistry, a western blot, a northern blot, a probe-based quantitative amplification assay, a whole genomic sequencing, a whole proteomic sequencing, RNA sequencing, a polymerase chain reaction (PCR) and a quantitative real-time polymerase chain reaction (qRT-PCR). 5. The method of claim 1, wherein the biological sample comprises tumour cells; tumor associated stromal cells; tumor associated fibroblasts; tumor associated macrophages; other immune related cells selected from tumour infiltrating lymphocytes including CD8+ T cells, MDSCs and Tregs; NK cells, circulating tumour DNA (ctDNA); circulating tumour cells and combination thereof. 6. A method for selecting a subject with castration resistant prostate cancer (CrPC) for a combination therapy comprising a selective dipeptidyl peptidase inhibitor and a PD-1 axis antagonist, said method comprising: (a) obtaining a biological sample from the subject with castration resistant prostate cancer (CrPC); (b) determining the amplification and/or expression level of the genes including selective dipeptidyl peptidases consisting of DPP-8, DPP-9 or FAP and programmed death ligand-1 (PDL-1) or CD274 in the biological sample obtained from the subject; and (c) selecting the subject as a suitable candidate for the said therapy if based on an assessment that the amplification and/or expression level of selective dipeptidyl peptidases consisting of DPP-8, DPP-9 or FAP and programmed death ligand-1 (PDL- 1) or CD274 exceeds a predetermined threshold level. 7. The method of claim 6 further compri sing the step of administering to the subject a therapeutically effective amount of the selective dipeptidyl peptidase inhibitor and the PD-1 axis antagonist. 8. The method of claim 6, wherein the determining the amplification and/or expression level of the genes comprises an amplification assay, a hybridization assay, an immunoassay, immunohistochemistry, a western blot, a northern blot, a whole genomic sequencing, a whole proteomic sequencing, RNA sequencing, a probe-based quantitative amplification assay, a polymerase chain reaction (PGR) or a quantitative real-time polymerase chain reaction (qRT- PCR). 9. The method of claim 6, wherein the castration resistant prostate cancer (CrPC) comprises small cell cancer, large cell cancer, neuroendocrine prostate cancer (NEPC), castration resistant prostate cancer neuroendocrine (CrPC-NE) and castration resistant prostate cancer (CrPC) pre- treated with abiraterone and/or enzalutamide. 10. The method of claim 6, wherein the biological sample comprises tumour cells; tumor associated stromal cells; tumor associated fibroblasts; tumor associated macrophages; other immune related cells selected from tumour infiltrating lymphocytes including CD8+ T cells, MDSCs and Tregs; NK cells, circulating tumour DNA (ctDNA); circulating tumour cells and combination thereof. 11. The method of claim 6, wherein the subject is human. 12. The method of claim 6, wherein the selective dipeptidyl peptidase inhibitor is talabostat or a pharmaceutically acceptable salt thereof. 13. The method of claim 6, wherein the PD-1 axis antagonist consisting of PD-1 antagonist, PDL-1 antagonist and PDL-2 antagonist. 14. The method of claim 13, wherein the PD-1 antagonist selected from the group consisting of ANA011, AUNP-12, BGB-A317, KD033, pembrolizumab, MCLA-134, mDX400, MEDI0680, muDX400, nivolumab, PDR001, PF-06801591, REGN-2810, SHR-1210, STI- Al 110, TSR-042, ANB011 and XCE853 and combination thereof. 15. The method of claim 13, wherein the PDL-1 antagonist selected from the group consisting of avelumab, BMS-936559, CA-170, durvalumab, MCLA-145, SP142, STI-A1011, STI- A1012, STI-AIOIO, STI-A1014 and atezolimumab and combination thereof. 16. The method of claim 13, wherein the PDL-2 antagonist consisting of AMP-224 and rHIgM12B7 and combination thereof. 17. A method for determining likelihood that a therapy involving administration of a selective dipeptidyl peptidase inhibitor and a PD-1 axis antagonist to a subject afflicted with castration resistant prostate cancer (CrPC) will provide a therapeutic benefit to the subject which comprises: (a) determining the amplification and/or expression level of the genes including selective dipeptidyl peptidases consisting of DPP-8, DPP-9 or FAP and programmed death ligand-1 (PDL-1) or CD274 in a biological sample obtained from the subject who is receiving the said therapy: (b) comparing the amplification and/or expression level of the genes including selective dipeptidyl peptidases consisting of DPP-8, DPP-9 or FAP and programmed death ligand-1 (PDL-1) or CD274 so obtained from the subject after receiving the said therapy to a predetermined threshold level of respective genes in the biological sample of subjects who had a lack of therapeutic benefit from such a therapy; wherein there is a likelihood that the therapy provides the therapeutic benefit to the subject if the level of amplification and/or expression determined in step a) is less than the predetermined threshold level. 18. The method of claim 17, wherein the determining the amplification and/or expression level of the genes comprise an amplification assay, a hybridization assay, an immunoassay. 

 immun ohistochemistr , a western blot, a northern blot, a whole genomic sequencing, a whole proteomic sequencing, RNA sequencing, a probe-based quantitative amplification assay, a polymerase chain reaction (PCR) and a quantitative real-time polymerase chain reaction (qRT- PCR). 19. The method of claim 17, wherein the castration resistant prostate cancer (CrPC) comprises small cell cancer, large cell cancer, neuroendocrine prostate cancer (NEPC), castration resistant prostate cancer neuroendocrine (CrPC-NE) and castration resistant prostate cancer (CrPC) pre- treated with abiraterone and/or enzalutamide. 20. The method of claim 17, wherein the biological sample comprises tumour cells; tumor associated stromal cells; tumor associated fibroblasts; tumor associated macrophages; other immune related cells selected from tumour infiltrating lymphocytes including CD8+ T cells, MDSCs and Tregs; NK cells, circulating tumour DNA (ctDNA); circulating tumour cells and combination thereof. 21. The method of claim 17, wherein the subject is human. 22. The method of claim 17, wherein the selective dipeptidyl peptidase inhibitor is talabostat or a pharmaceutically acceptable salt thereof. 23. The method of claim 17, wherein the PD-1 axis antagonist consisting of PD-1 antagonist, PDL-1 antagonist and PDL-2 antagonist. 24. The method of claim 23, wherein the PD-1 antagonist selected from the group consisting of ANA011, AUNP-12, BGB-A317, KD033, pembrolizumab, MCLA-134, mDX400, MED10680, muDX400, nivolumab, PDR001, PF-06801591, REGN-2810, SHR-1210, STI- Al 110, TSR-042, ANB011 and XCE853 and combination thereof. 25. The method of claim 23, wherein the PDL-1 antagonist selected from the group consisting of avelumab, BMS-936559, CA-170, durvalumab, MCLA-145, SP142, STI-A1011, STI- A1012, STI-A1010, STI-A1014 and atezolimumab and combination thereof. 26. The method of claim 23, wherein the PDL-2 antagonist consists of AMP-224 and rHIgM12B7 and combination thereof. 27. A method of prognosing castration resistant prostate cancer (CrPC) in a subject; said method comprising: 

 (a) determining in a subject the amplification and/or expression level of the genes including selective dipeptidyl peptidases consisting of DPP-8, DPP-9 or FAP and programmed death ligand-1 (PDL-1) or CD274 on a prognostic panel; (b) comparing the determined amplification and/or expression level of the genes including selective dipeptidyl peptidases consisting of DPP-8, DPP-9 or FAP and programmed death ligand-1 (PDL-1) or CD274 to a predetermined threshold level; and (c) prognosing a case of castration resistant prostate cancer (CrPC) if the determined amplification and/or expression level is higher than or exceeds the predetermined threshold level. 28. The method of claim 27, wherein the determining the expression or amplification level of the genes comprises an amplification assay, a hybridization assay, an immunoassay, immunohistochemistry, a western blot, a northern blot, a whole genomic sequencing, a whole proteomic sequencing, RNA sequencing, a probe-based quantitative amplification assay, a polymerase chain reaction (PCR) and a quantitative real-time polymerase chain reaction (qRT- PCR). 29. The method of claim 27, wherein the castration resistant prostate cancer (CrPC) comprises small cell cancer, large cell cancer, neuroendocrine prostate cancer (NEPC), castration resistant prostate cancer neuroendocrine (CrPC-NE) and castration resistant prostate cancer (CrPC) pre- treated with abiraterone and/or enzalutamide. 30. The method of claim 27, wherein the biological sample comprises tumour cells; tumor associated stromal cells; tumor associated fibroblasts; tumor associated macrophages; other immune related cells selected from tumour infiltrating lymphoc tes including CD8+ T cells, MDSCs and Tregs; NK cells, circulating tumour DNA (ctDNA); circulating tumour cells and combination thereof. 31. The method of claim 27, wherein the subject is human. 32. The method of claim 27, wherein the selective dipeptidyl peptidase inhibitor is talabostat or a pharmaceutically acceptable salt thereof. 33. The method of claim 27, wherein the PD-1 axis antagonist consisting of PD-1 antagonist, PDL-1 antagonist and PDL-2 antagonist. 34. The method of claim 33, wherein the PD-1 antagonist selected from the group consisting of ANA011, AUNP-12, BGB-A317, KD033, pembrolizumab, MCLA-134, mDX400, 

 MEDI0680, muDX400, nivolumab, PDR001, PF-06801591, REGN-2810, SHR-1210, STI- Al 110, TSR-042, ANBOl l and XCE853 and combination thereof. 35. The method of claim 33, wherein the PDL-1 antagonist selected from the group consisting of avelumab, BMS-936559, CA-170, durvalumab, MCLA-145, SP142, STI-A1011, STI- A1012, STI-A1010, STI-A1014 and atezolimumab and combination thereof. 36. The method of claim 33, wherein the PDL-2 antagonist consisting of AMP-224 and rHIgM12B7 and combination thereof.
